# Eurobase — Full API Reference

> EU-sovereign Backend-as-a-Service platform. The European alternative to Firebase and Supabase.
> This is the comprehensive reference for AI models. For a concise overview, see /llms.txt.

## Overview

Eurobase is a fully European-operated backend-as-a-service (BaaS) platform providing 10 integrated services:
authentication, managed PostgreSQL, object storage, realtime subscriptions, edge functions, encrypted vault,
cron jobs, webhooks, CLI tooling, and audit/compliance. All infrastructure runs on Scaleway (Paris, France) —
100% EU-owned, zero US CLOUD Act exposure, GDPR-native by design.

- Website: https://eurobase.app
- Console: https://console.eurobase.app
- Docs: https://docs.eurobase.app
- Status: Beta (public early access May 2026)
- SDK package: @eurobase/sdk (npm)
- CLI package: @eurobase/cli (npm)

---

## 1. SDK — @eurobase/sdk

Zero external dependencies. Works in browsers and Node.js 18+.

### Installation

```bash
npm install @eurobase/sdk
```

### Client Initialization

```typescript
import { createClient } from '@eurobase/sdk'

// Object config
const euro = createClient({
  url: 'https://my-app.eurobase.app',
  apiKey: 'eb_pk_...',
})

// Connection string format
const euro = createClient('eurobase://eb_pk_xxx@my-app.eurobase.app')
```

**Exports from @eurobase/sdk:**
- `createClient(config | connectionString)` — factory, returns EurobaseClient
- `EurobaseClient` — interface with `.auth`, `.db`, `.functions`, `.storage`, `.realtime`, `.vault`, `.status()`
- `EurobaseConfig` — `{ url: string, apiKey: string }`
- `QueryBuilder`, `DatabaseClient`, `QueryResult`
- `StorageClient`, `UploadResult`, `SignedUrlResult`, `ObjectInfo`, `ListResult`, `ListOptions`, `UploadOptions`, `SignedUrlOptions`
- `RealtimeClient`, `RealtimeEvent`, `SubscriptionCallback`, `Subscription`, `RealtimeEventType`
- `AuthClient`, `AuthUser`, `AuthSession`, `SignUpCredentials`, `SignInCredentials`, `AuthEvent`, `AuthStateChangeCallback`
- `FunctionsClient`, `FunctionInvokeOptions`, `FunctionError`
- `VaultClient`, `VaultSecret`, `VaultSecretMeta`
- `SchemaClient`, `ColumnDefinition`, `CreateTableOptions`, `CreateTableResult`, `RLSPreset`

### API Keys

Two key types:
- **Public key** (`eb_pk_*`) — safe for client-side code. Used for auth, database reads (with RLS), storage, realtime.
- **Secret key** (`eb_sk_*`) — server-side only. Required for vault, schema DDL, and bypassing RLS.

---

## 2. Authentication (euro.auth)

6 authentication methods. JWT-based sessions with automatic refresh.

### Methods

```typescript
// Email/password
auth.signUp({ email, password, metadata? }): Promise<{ data: AuthSession | null, error: string | null }>
auth.signIn({ email, password }): Promise<{ data: AuthSession | null, error: string | null }>
auth.signOut(): Promise<{ error: string | null }>
auth.refreshSession(): Promise<{ data: AuthSession | null, error: string | null }>

// Magic links (passwordless)
auth.requestMagicLink(email): Promise<{ error: string | null }>
auth.signInWithMagicLink(token): Promise<{ data: AuthSession | null, error: string | null }>

// OAuth (browser redirect flow)
auth.signInWithOAuth(provider, { redirectTo? }): void
auth.handleOAuthCallback(): { data: AuthSession | null, error: string | null }
// Supported providers: google, github, linkedin, apple, microsoft

// Phone SMS OTP — via REST API (see endpoints below)

// Session management
auth.getUser(): Promise<{ data: AuthUser | null, error: string | null }>
auth.getSession(): AuthSession | null
auth.onAuthStateChange(callback): () => void  // returns unsubscribe fn
```

### Types

```typescript
interface AuthUser {
  id: string
  email: string
  display_name?: string
  avatar_url?: string
  metadata?: Record<string, any>
  created_at: string
  updated_at: string
}

interface AuthSession {
  access_token: string
  token_type: string
  expires_in: number
  refresh_token: string
  user: AuthUser
}

type AuthEvent = 'SIGNED_IN' | 'SIGNED_OUT' | 'TOKEN_REFRESHED'
```

### Auth REST Endpoints

```
POST /v1/auth/signup              — Email/password sign-up
POST /v1/auth/signin              — Email/password sign-in
POST /v1/auth/signout             — Sign out (revoke refresh token)
POST /v1/auth/refresh             — Refresh access token
POST /v1/auth/forgot-password     — Request password reset email
POST /v1/auth/reset-password      — Reset password with token
POST /v1/auth/verify-email        — Verify email with token
POST /v1/auth/resend-verification — Resend verification email
POST /v1/auth/request-magic-link  — Send magic link email
POST /v1/auth/signin-magic-link   — Sign in with magic link token
GET  /v1/auth/oauth/{provider}    — Initiate OAuth redirect
GET  /v1/auth/oauth/{provider}/callback — OAuth callback
POST /v1/auth/phone/send-otp      — Send SMS OTP
POST /v1/auth/phone/verify        — Verify SMS OTP
GET  /v1/auth/user                — Get current user (requires JWT)
```

---

## 3. Database (euro.db)

Managed PostgreSQL with a fluent query builder. PostgREST-style query parameters.

### Query Builder

```typescript
// Start a query chain
const query = euro.db.from<Type>('table_name')

// Column selection
.select('col1', 'col2', ...)

// Filters
.eq(column, value)      // column = value
.neq(column, value)     // column != value
.gt(column, value)      // column > value
.gte(column, value)     // column >= value
.lt(column, value)      // column < value
.lte(column, value)     // column <= value
.like(column, pattern)  // LIKE (case-sensitive)
.ilike(column, pattern) // ILIKE (case-insensitive)
.in(column, values[])   // IN (value1, value2, ...)

// Ordering & Pagination
.order(column, { ascending?: boolean })
.limit(count)
.offset(count)
.single()  // expect exactly one row, unwraps array

// Awaitable — implements PromiseLike
const { data, count, error } = await query
```

### Write Operations

```typescript
// Insert
await euro.db.from('todos').insert({ title: 'Buy milk', user_id: 'uuid' })

// Update by ID
await euro.db.from('todos').update('row-uuid', { done: true })

// Delete by ID
await euro.db.from('todos').delete('row-uuid')

// Raw SQL (via REST)
POST /v1/db/sql  — body: { query: "SELECT ...", params: [] }
POST /v1/db/rpc/{function_name} — call a stored procedure
```

### Schema DDL (requires secret key eb_sk_*)

```typescript
const schema = euro.db.schema

// Create table with RLS
await schema.createTable('posts', {
  columns: [
    { name: 'id', type: 'uuid', primary_key: true, default_value: 'gen_random_uuid()' },
    { name: 'title', type: 'text' },
    { name: 'body', type: 'text', nullable: true },
    { name: 'user_id', type: 'uuid' },  // auto-detected as owner column
  ],
  rlsPreset: 'owner_access',  // optional, auto-detected
})

// Drop table
await schema.dropTable('posts')

// Add column
await schema.addColumn('posts', { name: 'published', type: 'boolean', default_value: 'false' })

// Drop column
await schema.dropColumn('posts', 'published')
```

### RLS Presets

- `owner_access` — Owner can CRUD their own rows (matched by user_id/owner_id/created_by column)
- `public_read_owner_write` — Anyone can read, owner can write
- `authenticated_read_owner_write` — Authenticated users can read, owner can write
- `full_access` — Any authenticated user can CRUD all rows
- `read_only` — Any authenticated user can read, no writes
- `none` — No preset applied (manual RLS setup)

### Database REST Endpoints

```
GET    /v1/db/{table}            — Query rows (supports PostgREST-style params)
GET    /v1/db/{table}/{id}       — Get row by ID
POST   /v1/db/{table}            — Insert row
PATCH  /v1/db/{table}/{id}       — Update row
DELETE /v1/db/{table}/{id}       — Delete row
POST   /v1/db/{table}/bulk-delete — Bulk delete
POST   /v1/db/sql                — Raw SQL query
POST   /v1/db/rpc/{function}     — Call stored procedure
POST   /v1/db/schema/tables/     — Create table (secret key)
DELETE /v1/db/schema/tables/{name} — Drop table (secret key)
POST   /v1/db/schema/tables/{name}/columns — Add column (secret key)
DELETE /v1/db/schema/tables/{name}/columns/{col} — Drop column (secret key)
```

---

## 4. Storage (euro.storage)

S3-compatible object storage with signed URL support.

### Methods

```typescript
// Upload — supports File, Blob, ArrayBuffer, Uint8Array, Node.js Buffer
storage.upload(key, file, { contentType? }): Promise<UploadResult>

// Download
storage.download(key): Promise<Blob>

// Delete
storage.remove(key): Promise<{ error: string | null }>

// List files
storage.list({ prefix?, limit?, cursor? }): Promise<ListResult>

// Signed URLs (upload or download)
storage.createSignedUrl(key, 'upload' | 'download', { expiresIn?, contentType? }): Promise<SignedUrlResult>
```

### Types

```typescript
interface UploadResult { key: string, content_type: string, size: number, error: string | null }
interface SignedUrlResult { url: string, expires_at: string, error: string | null }
interface ObjectInfo { key: string, size: number, last_modified: string, content_type?: string }
interface ListResult { objects: ObjectInfo[], next_cursor: string | null, has_more: boolean, error: string | null }
```

### Storage REST Endpoints

```
POST   /v1/storage/upload       — Upload file (multipart/form-data)
GET    /v1/storage/{key}        — Download file
DELETE /v1/storage/{key}        — Delete file
GET    /v1/storage              — List files (?prefix=&limit=&cursor=)
POST   /v1/storage/signed-url   — Generate signed URL
```

---

## 5. Realtime (euro.realtime)

WebSocket subscriptions for database change events. Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff.

### Methods

```typescript
// Subscribe to table events
const sub = euro.realtime.on(table, event, callback)
// event: 'INSERT' | 'UPDATE' | 'DELETE' | '*'

// Unsubscribe
euro.realtime.off(sub)

// Disconnect
euro.realtime.disconnect()
```

### Types

```typescript
interface RealtimeEvent {
  channel: string      // e.g. "db:orders"
  type: string         // "INSERT" | "UPDATE" | "DELETE"
  record: any          // new row data
  old_record?: any     // previous row data (on UPDATE/DELETE)
  timestamp: string
}
```

### WebSocket Endpoint

```
GET /v1/realtime?token={jwt_or_apikey}  — WebSocket upgrade
// Messages: { action: "subscribe", channel: "db:table_name" }
```

---

## 6. Vault (euro.vault)

AES-256-GCM encrypted key-value secrets store. Requires secret API key (eb_sk_*).

### Methods

```typescript
vault.list(): Promise<{ data: VaultSecretMeta[] | null, error: string | null }>
vault.get(name): Promise<{ data: string | null, error: string | null }>
vault.set(name, value, description?): Promise<{ error: string | null }>
vault.delete(name): Promise<{ error: string | null }>
```

### Types

```typescript
interface VaultSecretMeta { name: string, description: string, created_at: string, updated_at: string }
interface VaultSecret extends VaultSecretMeta { id: string, value: string }
```

### Vault REST Endpoints

```
GET    /v1/vault           — List secrets (metadata only)
GET    /v1/vault/{name}    — Get decrypted secret
POST   /v1/vault           — Create/update secret
DELETE /v1/vault/{name}    — Delete secret
```

---

## 7. Edge Functions (euro.functions)

Serverless TypeScript/JavaScript functions on Deno runtime.

### SDK Method

```typescript
functions.invoke<T>(name, { body?, method?, headers? }): Promise<{ data: T | null, error: FunctionError | null }>
```

### Functions REST Endpoints

```
POST /v1/functions/{name}  — Invoke function (SDK route)

# Platform management endpoints (require platform JWT):
GET    /platform/projects/{id}/functions/          — List functions
POST   /platform/projects/{id}/functions/          — Create function
GET    /platform/projects/{id}/functions/{name}    — Get function details
PUT    /platform/projects/{id}/functions/{name}    — Update function code
DELETE /platform/projects/{id}/functions/{name}    — Delete function
GET    /platform/projects/{id}/functions/{name}/logs     — Get execution logs
GET    /platform/projects/{id}/functions/{name}/triggers — List triggers
POST   /platform/projects/{id}/functions/{name}/triggers — Create trigger
DELETE /platform/projects/{id}/functions/{name}/triggers/{id} — Delete trigger
GET    /platform/projects/{id}/functions/{name}/versions — List versions
POST   /platform/projects/{id}/functions/{name}/rollback — Rollback to version
GET    /platform/projects/{id}/functions/{name}/metrics  — Get metrics
```

---

## 8. Cron Jobs

Schedule SQL queries and RPC calls with execution logging.

### Cron REST Endpoints (platform-authenticated)

```
GET    /platform/projects/{id}/cron     — List cron jobs
POST   /platform/projects/{id}/cron     — Create cron job
GET    /platform/projects/{id}/cron/{jobId} — Get job details
PUT    /platform/projects/{id}/cron/{jobId} — Update job
DELETE /platform/projects/{id}/cron/{jobId} — Delete job
```

---

## 9. Webhooks

Event-driven HTTP delivery with retry logic and HMAC signing.

### Webhooks REST Endpoints (platform-authenticated)

```
GET    /platform/projects/{id}/webhooks     — List webhooks
POST   /platform/projects/{id}/webhooks     — Create webhook
GET    /platform/projects/{id}/webhooks/{whId} — Get webhook details
PUT    /platform/projects/{id}/webhooks/{whId} — Update webhook
DELETE /platform/projects/{id}/webhooks/{whId} — Delete webhook
```

---

## 10. Platform & Project Management

### Project (Tenant) Management

```
POST   /v1/tenants       — Create project
GET    /v1/tenants        — List projects
PATCH  /v1/tenants/{id}   — Update project
DELETE /v1/tenants/{id}   — Delete project
```

### Platform Auth (Console Authentication)

```
POST /platform/auth/signup           — Sign up for platform account
POST /platform/auth/signin           — Sign in to platform
POST /platform/auth/forgot-password  — Request password reset
POST /platform/auth/reset-password   — Reset password
GET  /platform/auth/account/profile  — Get account profile
PATCH /platform/auth/account/profile — Update profile
POST /platform/auth/account/change-password — Change password
POST /platform/auth/account/delete   — Delete account
```

### Team Management

```
GET    /platform/projects/{id}/members           — List team members
POST   /platform/projects/{id}/members/invite    — Invite member
POST   /platform/projects/{id}/members/resend    — Resend invitation
DELETE /platform/projects/{id}/members/{userId}  — Remove member
PATCH  /platform/projects/{id}/members/{userId}  — Change role
POST   /platform/invitations/accept              — Accept invitation
```

### Schema Introspection

```
GET /platform/projects/{id}/schema         — Full schema introspection
GET /platform/projects/{id}/schema/changes — Schema change history
GET /platform/projects/{id}/schema/rls-audit — RLS policy audit
```

### End-User Management (Console)

```
GET /platform/projects/{id}/users  — List end-users for a project
```

### API Keys

```
GET  /platform/projects/{id}/api-keys            — List API keys
POST /platform/projects/{id}/api-keys/regenerate  — Regenerate keys
GET  /platform/projects/{id}/connect              — Get connection info
```

### Compliance

```
GET /platform/projects/{id}/compliance/dpa-report      — Generate DPA report
GET /platform/projects/{id}/compliance/sub-processors   — Sub-processor registry
GET /platform/projects/{id}/compliance/audit-log        — Audit log
```

### Email Templates

```
GET    /platform/projects/{id}/email-templates           — List templates
PUT    /platform/projects/{id}/email-templates/{type}    — Update template
DELETE /platform/projects/{id}/email-templates/{type}    — Reset to default
POST   /platform/projects/{id}/email-templates/{type}/preview — Preview
POST   /platform/projects/{id}/email-templates/{type}/test    — Send test
```

### Plan & Usage

```
GET /platform/config/plans                — Get available plans
GET /platform/projects/{id}/usage         — Get usage stats
```

### Health

```
GET /health — Returns { "status": "ok" }
```

---

## 11. Plan Limits

| Feature | Free | Pro |
|---------|------|-----|
| Database size | 500 MB | 5 GB |
| Storage | 1 GB | 50 GB |
| Bandwidth | 5 GB | 100 GB |
| Monthly active users | 10,000 | 100,000 |
| Rate limit | 100 req/s | 1,000 req/s |
| WebSocket connections | 100 | 10,000 |
| Upload file size | 10 MB | 50 MB |
| Webhooks | 3 | Unlimited |
| Projects | 2 | 10 |
| Log retention | 1 day | 30 days |
| Custom email templates | No | Yes |

Enterprise plan: custom limits, dedicated infrastructure, SLA, compliance tooling.

---

## 12. Architecture

- **Gateway**: Go (chi router), handles REST API, WebSocket, rate limiting, auth middleware
- **Database**: PostgreSQL with per-tenant schemas, row-level security
- **Storage**: S3-compatible (Scaleway Object Storage)
- **Edge Functions**: Deno runtime, managed by functions-runner service
- **Vault**: AES-256-GCM encryption, keys stored in Scaleway KMS
- **Realtime**: WebSocket hub with pub/sub event routing
- **Infrastructure**: Scaleway Kubernetes (Paris, France)
- **DNS**: EU-managed DNS
- **SDK**: TypeScript, zero dependencies, browser + Node.js 18+
- **CLI**: Node.js, 30+ commands

### Security

- All traffic over TLS 1.3
- Security headers: HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, CSP
- Rate limiting per IP and per API key
- Row-level security with policy presets
- AES-256-GCM vault encryption
- HMAC-signed webhooks
- JWT tokens with configurable expiry
- Audit logging with actor, action, IP, timestamp

### Compliance

- GDPR-native architecture
- All data stored in EU (France)
- Zero US CLOUD Act exposure
- Schrems II compliant
- Automated DPA reports
- Sub-processor registry
- GDPR data export
- Full audit trail
- EU-owned infrastructure provider (Scaleway)

---

## 13. MCP Server (Claude Code Integration)

Connect Eurobase to Claude Code for AI-assisted development:

```bash
claude mcp add eurobase https://mcp.eurobase.app/mcp --header "Authorization: Bearer <your-platform-token>"
```

Available tools: listProjects, getProject, listTables, describeTable, runSQL, listUsers, listFiles, getSignedUrl, listSecrets, getSecret, setSecret, listFunctions, invokeFunction, createTable, healthCheck.

---

## 14. Comparison with Alternatives

### vs Supabase
- Same Postgres foundation, but Eurobase is 100% EU infrastructure (vs AWS us-east-1)
- No CLOUD Act exposure (Supabase is US-incorporated, data subject to US law)
- Native DPA reports and audit logging (Supabase requires manual setup)
- No free tier project pausing (Supabase pauses after 7 days of inactivity)
- Integrated vault, cron, webhooks in base platform

### vs Firebase
- SQL (PostgreSQL) vs NoSQL (Firestore) — no proprietary query language
- No vendor lock-in — standard Postgres, S3 storage, REST APIs
- EU infrastructure vs Google Cloud (US jurisdiction)
- Open data format — export anytime via pg_dump
- Row-level security via SQL policies, not proprietary rules

---

- Website: https://eurobase.app
- Console: https://console.eurobase.app
- Docs: https://docs.eurobase.app
